semanticCore: Leaves, paper, silver coins, currency, foliage, or trace
quranicRelevance: Silver coins/currency (Wariq - 'bi-wariqikum'), and leaves covering Adam and Eve ('waraqi al-jannati').

keyDerivations.meaning: Silver coins, minted silver, currency, or cash
Leaves, foliage, green sheets, or paper sheets

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَابْعَثُوا أَحَدَكُمْ بِوَرِقِكُمْ هَٰذِهِ إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ (18:19): So send one of you with this silver currency/coin of yours to the city (the sleepers' ancient cash).
discardedNoise: Green leaf salads in culinary menus.
summaryEn: The root represents silver cash (Wariq) and tree leaves (Waraq), detailing the ancient coins of the cave sleepers and the Paradise leaves used by Adam.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر النقد الفضي الورق وأوراق الشجر، واصفاً العملة الفضية القديمة لأصحاب الكهف (بورقكم)، وورق الجنة الساتر لآدم وحواء.
rawText: أَوْرَاق , pl. of وَرَقٌ, meaning أَحْدَاث: see قَعْبٌ. أَوْرَقُ , applied to a camel, White inclining to black; i. e. of a dusky white hue: or rather, simply, duskish; or dusky; (S, K;) or of a colour like that of ashes. (T, Mgh, Msb.) See أَحْمَرُ and خُطَبَانِىٌّ. === أَوْرَقُ Ashes. (K.) See an ex. a verse cited voce عُنَّةٌ, last sentence. شَجَرٌ وَارِقٌ Trees having leaves: (Msb:) [or leafy trees; trees having many leaves; for] شَجَرَةٌ وَارِقَةٌ (TA) and 🡻وَرِقَةٌ and 🡻وَرِيقَةٌ (S, K, TA) signify a tree having many leaves. (S, K, TA.) And شَجَرَةٌ وَارِقَةُ الظِّلَالِ [A tree having leafy coverings or shades]. (K in art. غيل.) وَرِقٌ Silver, whether coined or not: (AO, TA:) or coined dirhems; (S, K;) coined silver. (Mgh.) See عَيْنٌ. وَرِقَةٌ and وَرِيقَةٌ: see وَارِقٌ. وُرْقَةٌ Ash-colour. (Msb.) See سُمْرَةٌ.
